input.thought: Open official Close developer, pricing, and GitHub/MCP-related pages to verify API auth, access, rate limits, and integration signals.

arguments.task: Research official Close developer documentation. Find pages that state authentication methods for the API, API reference surface, and rate limits. Return exact source URLs and short quoted supporting text. Also check whether the docs mention GraphQL or only REST.

Research official Close pricing or access information. Find whether Close offers self-serve sign-up and whether API access appears available on standard plans or requires sales/admin approval. Return exact source URLs and short quoted supporting text.

Research whether an MCP server exists for Close. Prefer official Close GitHub or official docs; if none, check GitHub search results and MCP registry-style listings. Return exact source URLs and whether the MCP appears official or community-maintained.

The API key acts as the username and the password is always empty.\\\\\\\"\\n- **OAuth 2.0**\\n  - **URL:** [https://developer.close.com/api/overview/oauth-authentication](https://developer.close.com/api/overview/oauth-authentication)\\n  - **Supporting Text:** \\\\\\\"Send your HTTP requests with an `Authorization` header that contains the word `Bearer` followed by a space and the Access Token.\\\\\\\"\\n\\n### 2. raw_result.description: CRM and sales engagement platform with API access for core CRM, communications, and automation workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs support OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens and API keys sent via HTTP Basic auth where the API key is the username and password is empty; official MCP docs also indicate API-key auth with MCP scopes.
raw_result.gating_reason: Close pricing and FAQ indicate self-serve signup and trial without talking to sales.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API at https://api.close.com/api/v1 covering CRM core objects, activities, events/webhooks, search/reporting, plus an official remote MCP server for agent access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Close MCP server is documented at help.close.com/docs/mcp-server with endpoint https://mcp.close.com/mcp; a community MCP server also exists on GitHub.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs say rate limits are enforced per endpoint group, at the organization level across all users' API keys, with an additional lower per-API-key limit. Most responses include a ratelimit header, and 429 responses include ratelimit and retry-after headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly state the API uses REST conventions; pricing indicates API access is included on standard plans and self-serve signup is available; official MCP support for Claude and ChatGPT is a strong agent-readiness signal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Pricing-task output contained conflicting detail about which plans include API access, and the docs evidence gathered was summarized by the browser rather than manually cross-checked line-by-line here; access-tier specifics and whether Solo includes API access should be confirmed by a human if plan granularity matters.

result.description: CRM and sales engagement platform with API access for core CRM, communications, and automation workflows.
result.auth_notes: Official docs support OAuth 2.0 bearer tokens and API keys sent via HTTP Basic auth where the API key is the username and password is empty; official MCP docs also indicate API-key auth with MCP scopes.
result.gating_reason: Close pricing and FAQ indicate self-serve signup and trial without talking to sales.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API at https://api.close.com/api/v1 covering CRM core objects, activities, events/webhooks, search/reporting, plus an official remote MCP server for agent access.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Close MCP server is documented at help.close.com/docs/mcp-server with endpoint https://mcp.close.com/mcp; a community MCP server also exists on GitHub.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official docs say rate limits are enforced per endpoint group, at the organization level across all users' API keys, with an additional lower per-API-key limit. Most responses include a ratelimit header, and 429 responses include ratelimit and retry-after headers.
result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly state the API uses REST conventions; pricing indicates API access is included on standard plans and self-serve signup is available; official MCP support for Claude and ChatGPT is a strong agent-readiness signal.
